title: cornus stolonifera var. occidentalis
intro: Cornus stolonifera var. occidentalis, also known as the Red Osier Dogwood or Western Red Osier Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to western North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The medicinal properties of Cornus stolonifera var. occidentalis are believed to promote overall health and well-being, with potential benefits including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions and promote skin and wound healing.
